# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { openKV } from 'https://deno.land/std@0.165.0/flags/mod.ts';
import { KV_NAMESPACE } from '../env.ts';

export async function searchUsers(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const params = new URLSearchParams(req.url);
  const filters: string[] = [];
  const userId = req.params.get('id');

  if (params.has('name')) {
    filters.push(`name="${params.get('name")}'`);
  }

  if (params.has('email')) {
    filters.push(`email="${params.get('email")}'`);
  }

  const namespace = KV_NAMESPACE;
  const store = await openKV({ name: namespace });

  for (const filter of filters) {
    const key = `users/${userId}/${filter}`;
    const value = await store.get(key);

    if (value !== null) {
      return new Response(JSON.stringify(value), {
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
      });
    }
  }

  return new Response(null, { status: 404 });
}
